content.text: Answer seven problems. (If you turn in more, the first seven will be graded.) Within reason, you may quote theorems as long as you state them clearly.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Show that for all \(n \geq 0\) there exists an irreducible polynomial \(f \in \mathbb{Q}[x]\) of degree greater than \(n\) that does not satisfy the hypotheses of Eisenstein’s criterion.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give an example, with proofs, of an irreducible polynomial which is not separable.

problem.text: Let \(n \geq 1\) and let \(F\) be a splitting field of \(x^n-1 \in \mathbb{Q}[x]\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that the Galois group of \(F\) over \(\mathbb{Q}\) is abelian.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: When \(n=8\) show that the Galois group is of order \(4\) and not cyclic.

problem.text: Prove that the Galois group of \(x^5-4x+2 \in \mathbb{Q}[x]\) is the symmetric group of degree \(5\).
Let \(F\) be a free group on a finite set \(X\) of size \(n\). Prove that the quotient of \(F\) by its commutator subgroup is isomorphic to \(\mathbb{Z}^n\).

Let \(R\) be a ring with \(1\) and let
\[
0 \to A \xrightarrow{f} B \xrightarrow{g} C \to 0
\]
be a short exact sequence of (left) \(R\)-modules. Show that for any (right) \(R\)-module \(M\), the sequence
\[
M \otimes_R A \xrightarrow{1^M \otimes f} M \otimes_R B \xrightarrow{1^M \otimes g} M \otimes_R C \to 0
\]
of tensor products, is exact.
Let \(F\) be a field and \(F[[x]]\) the ring of formal power series in the indeterminate \(x\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that \(F[[x]]\) is a principal ideal domain.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Determine all of the prime ideals of \(F[[x]]\).

problem.text: All parts involve ideals in a commutative ring with identity. Let \(S\) be a multiplicative set in \(R\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(I\) is an ideal of \(R\) then \(S^{-1}I=\left\{\frac{a}{s}\mid a\in I, s\in S\right\}\) is an ideal in the ring \(S^{-1}R\), the localization of \(R\) with respect to \(S\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Define the radical \(\operatorname{Rad} I\) of an ideal \(I\) and show that it is an ideal.

problem.text: You may not answer this problem just by quoting Nakayama’s Lemma. Let \(R\) be a commutative local ring with \(1\) and \(J\) its unique maximal ideal.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that \(1-a\) is a unit for every element \(a\) of \(J\),

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Let \(M\) be an \(R\)-module, with generators \(x_1,x_2,x_3\) and suppose that \(x_2\) and \(x_3\) lie in \(JM\). Show that \(M=Rx_1\).

problem.text: Let \(R\) be a ring with \(1\). All modules are unitary.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define the concept of a projective \(R\)-module.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Show that if \(P\) is a projective \(R\)-module, then there is an \(R\)-module \(K\) such that \(P\oplus K\) is isomorphic to a free module.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Show that for every nonzero element \(x\) of a free \(\mathbb{Z}\)-module \(F\) there is a homomorphism from \(F\) to a finite group such that the image of \(x\) is nontrivial.

subparts.label: (d)
subparts.text: Prove that \(\mathbb{Q}\) is not a projective \(\mathbb{Z}\)-module. (Hint: Show that \(\mathbb{Q}\) has no nontrivial finite quotients. Then use the earlier parts.)

problem.text: Let \(D\) be a division ring with center \(K\) and suppose \(a\) and \(b\) are elements of \(D\) which are algebraic over \(K\) and have the same minimum polynomial. Prove that \(b=dad^{-1}\) for some nonzero element \(d\in D\).
Let \(\mathcal{C}\) be a category and let \((A_i)_{i\in I}\) be a family of objects of \(\mathcal{C}\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define what is a coproduct of \((A_i)_{i\in I}\) in \(\mathcal{C}\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that in the category of sets the coproduct of two sets is their disjoint union.
